payload.description:
# Build stub PlexusModule for the spike

## What

A minimal Python module that, when registered into a TactusRuntime, exposes the read-only subset of the v1 Lua API surface needed for the spike tasks. Stubs only — backed by hand-curated fixture data, not real Plexus services.

## Surface to stub

- `plexus.scorecards.list`, `plexus.scorecards.info`
- `plexus.score.info`, `plexus.score.predict`, `plexus.score.evaluations`
- `plexus.feedback.find`, `plexus.feedback.alignment`
- `plexus.evaluation.info`, `plexus.evaluation.find_recent`
- `plexus.item.info`, `plexus.item.last`
- `plexus.task.info`, `plexus.task.last`
- `plexus.docs.list`, `plexus.docs.get`
- `plexus.budget.remaining`, `plexus.budget.with`
- `plexus.cost.analysis`

## Stubbing approach

- Backed by JSON fixture files under `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/fixtures/`.
- Realistic data shapes — must match what the real PlexusModule will eventually return so the spike validates against the actual API.
- Predictable / idempotent so test runs are reproducible.

## Constraints

- Stub must be invokable from sandboxed Tactus Lua via `local plexus = require('plexus')`. Requires the host-module mechanism from Epic 2 (or a temporary in-spike workaround that mutates `package.preload` directly via Lupa). Decision: prefer waiting for Epic 2's API; if it's not ready, use the workaround and document the tech-debt.
- Stubbed `plexus.evaluation.run` lives in a separate task (next).

## Deliverable

`spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py` plus fixtures, registerable into a TactusRuntime.
payload.title: Build stub PlexusModule for the spike (read-only operations)
